findings.description: Three SQL subqueries used hardcoded 'AND archived = 0' instead of the ACTIVE_FILTER constant from chat-db.ts. This violates DRY and the single-point-of-change design documented in chat-db.ts.

clearLastUserMessage is called unconditionally even when targeting a specific CLI tool (targetCliTool). If other CLI tools still have active messages, their last_user_message context is lost. However, since last_user_message is a worktree-level field (not per-tool), this is acceptable for now - clearing stale data is safer than showing incorrect data.

ACTIVE_FILTER constant is well-designed and consistently used across all chat-db.ts queries. The deleteAllMessages function correctly uses ACTIVE_FILTER to avoid re-archiving already archived messages.

Test suite is comprehensive with 15 tests covering all key scenarios: create, archive, filter, includeArchived, per-CLI-tool archiving, clearLastUserMessage, CASCADE deletion, and idempotent re-archiving.
Toggle UI for showArchived is clean and follows the existing pattern. Conditional rendering via onShowArchivedChange guard is correct.

changes_made.description: Imported ACTIVE_FILTER from chat-db.ts and replaced 3 hardcoded 'AND archived = 0' with ${ACTIVE_FILTER} interpolation in getLastMessagesByCliBatch, getWorktrees, and getWorktreeById subqueries. This ensures a single point of change for the archived filter clause.

quality_checks.tests: pass (1 pre-existing failure in git-utils.test.ts unrelated to Issue #168; all 15 db-archived-messages tests pass)
